rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the core migration logic and both independently surface the exact latent gap (the unmigrated hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 diffusion caller of get_cache_scale), so gap_hit is true for both. Y goes further by verifying the design against the actual upstream vLLM source (AutoWeightsLoader/fp8.py/weight_utils.py with line numbers) and lands a sharp, technically valid critique that the delegated-loader test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader and never proves the outer mapping happens — closer in spirit to lishunyang12's 'test doesn't prove real end-to-end resolution' concern than X's finding. X's review is solid and well-organized but its extra minor comments (missing comments on two blocks) are low-value nitpicks, and Y's claim that the diffusion gap was 'since addressed... see #4810' is a shaky, possibly fabricated specific that slightly dents its precision. Overall Y edges out X on recall and actionability at a small cost to precision.
